income_thresholds.description: No income threshold — eligibility based on employment status (central govt employee/pensioner/judge/MP), not income
income_thresholds.source: CGHS Rules / Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

exclusion_rules.description: Only central government employees, pensioners, and specified constitutional functionaries eligible
Employees who resigned without pension entitlement are not eligible
State government employees are not covered; they have separate state health schemes

family_criteria.family_definition: Covers employee/pensioner, spouse, dependent children (until age 25 or marriage), and dependent parents if ordinarily residing with the beneficiary. Dependent disabled children covered without age limit.

covered_procedures: OPD consultation at CGHS dispensaries and empanelled clinics
OPD medicines from CGHS dispensary or empanelled chemist
Hospitalization at empanelled private hospitals (as per CGHS rates)
Orthopedic (knee replacement, hip replacement, spinal surgery)
Organ transplant (liver, renal, corneal, bone marrow)
Diagnostic tests and imaging (X-ray, CT, MRI, PET scan)
AYUSH treatment (Ayurveda, Yoga, Unani, Siddha, Homeopathy)
excluded_procedures: Fertility treatment and IVF (limited coverage available)
Treatment at non-empanelled private hospitals (except emergencies)

required_documents.name: CGHS Card / e-Card
alternatives: CGHS Beneficiary ID with Aadhaar verification
required_documents.notes: Plastic CGHS card or e-card issued by CGHS; required for all OPD and hospitalization services

required_documents.name: Service ID / Pension Payment Order
alternatives: Last Pay Certificate for new pensioners
required_documents.local_name_hi: सेवा पहचान पत्र / पेंशन भुगतान आदेश
required_documents.notes: Government employee service card or PPO for pensioners; establishes employment eligibility

required_documents.name: Referral Letter from CGHS
alternatives: Emergency admission — referral obtained within 3 days
required_documents.notes: Referral from CGHS Wellness Centre/dispensary needed for specialist or hospital treatment

required_documents.name: Mobile Number
required_documents.notes: For appointment booking and scheme communications

required_documents.name: Dependency Certificate
required_documents.notes: For adding dependent family members; issued by the employing department

enrollment_steps: Step 1: Serving employees — submit CGHS application through employing ministry/department HR
Step 2: For pensioners — apply at nearest CGHS city office with PPO and Aadhaar within 3 months of retirement
Step 3: Submit family member details and dependency certificates
Step 4: Monthly contribution deducted from salary/pension (Rs 250-1000 based on pay level)
Step 5: CGHS plastic card / e-card issued for beneficiary and each dependent
Step 6: Register at preferred CGHS Wellness Centre for primary OPD care
Step 7: For specialist/hospital treatment, obtain referral from CGHS Wellness Centre doctor
description_for_embedding: Central Government Health Scheme (CGHS) is India's oldest and most comprehensive government healthcare scheme covering central government employees, pensioners, Members of Parliament, Supreme Court and High Court judges, freedom fighters, and accredited journalists. Unlike insurance-based schemes, CGHS provides direct healthcare through its network of Wellness Centres (dispensaries), government hospitals, and empanelled private hospitals in 83+ cities across India. Covers OPD, specialist consultation, hospitalization, diagnostics, medicines, dental, AYUSH, and rehabilitation. Beneficiaries pay a nominal monthly contribution from salary/pension. Referral from CGHS Wellness Centre required for specialist treatment. No coverage cap for government hospital treatment; empanelled private hospital rates capped at CGHS schedule.
